user_story: As a collection manager, I want to manage cataloger permissions for both collections and individual items, so that I can control who has access to edit, create, update, or delete records within my collection.
true_user_story: As a collection manager or cataloger I want to be able to see who last edited a record and when.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to add or remove catalogers from collections and items, so that I can control who has access to edit records within my collection.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has create/update/delete access to records in my collection.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to view and manage the list of catalogers associated with my collections and items, so that I can ensure proper access control and collaboration within my team.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has edit access to individual records in my collection.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to view and manage the metadata profiles associated with my collections, so that I can ensure accurate and consistent cataloging across all records in my collection.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict access to collection metadata.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to monitor the progress of collections in real-time, so that I can effectively manage and facilitate the execution of projects.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to edit an arbitrary number of Items at once so that I can make changes to all Items in a collection efficiently.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to add or remove catalogers from a Collection or an Item, so that I can efficiently manage access and permissions for different projects.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to use a grid/spreadsheet style interface so I can edit Items quickly.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to be able to view incomplete items with missing structural or descriptive metadata records, so that I can ensure all necessary information is included before saving or publishing the item.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to save a record without all of the required metadata so that I can start a record with the metadata I have and come back to finish it later.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to add items to a collection without completing all required metadata fields, so that I can start organizing my records while still having the flexibility to complete them later.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to save a record only when the record has an identifier so that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.

user_story: As a Limited Cataloger, I want to be able to save an incomplete item without all required metadata so that I can return later to complete it, ensuring that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to publish records only when the record has a Title, Identifier, Type, and

user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager, I want to be able to filter records based on their status and Duke Core fields, so that I can efficiently manage and track the progress of items within specific collections.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want a list of all of the records I have access to so I can get to work quickly.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to filter records based on metadata fields, so that I can efficiently monitor collection progress and verify metadata records.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to search for records based on status so I can track the progress of a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to filter incomplete items by collection or category, so that I can efficiently manage and complete my assigned metadata records.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to search for records based on all Duke Core fields.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to filter incomplete items within any collection or search result, so that I can efficiently manage and complete pending tasks.
true_user_story: As an end user I want to be able to search across all collections at once.

user_story: As a collections manager, I want to be able to create/update/delete collections, so that I can manage the metadata structure of my repository effectively.
true_user_story: As a cataloger and a collections manager I want Trident to support Duke Core so that metadata is consistent between collections.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to select from predefined authority lists for metadata fields, so that the metadata is consistent and validated according to Duke Core standards.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want required DC fields to be enforced so that all records have values for all required DC fields. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to add authority lists for metadata fields in a collection, so that the metadata is consistent and validated according to Duke Core standards.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want to be able to repeat repeatable DC Fields so that I can enter all of the relevant metadata. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want Trident to use authority lists for metadata fields, so that the metadata records are consistent and accurate.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want Optional DC fields to be available but not required so that my collection can decide to use them or not. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to select from predefined authority lists for metadata fields when creating or editing DMRs, so that the metadata records are consistent and standardized across the repository.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to be able to create controlled vocabularies so that they can be used as authority lists during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to select from predefined authority lists when creating or updating metadata records, so that the consistency and accuracy of the repository's data are maintained.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want authority lists to be easily accessible and contextually relevant so it is easy to use the authority.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to select from predefined authority lists for metadata fields when creating or editing DMRs, so that the metadata records are consistent and standardized across the repository.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to use external authority lists so I they can be used during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to view incomplete items within a collection, so that I can prioritize and complete them efficiently.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read the digital object I am cataloging so I don't have to move between multiple applications while I'm creating metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager, I want to be able to view incomplete items within the digital repository, so that I can quickly identify and address any missing components or metadata before finalizing the item.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read all components associated on the with an item on the create/update screen so I don't have to leave the form.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to view incomplete items within a collection, so that I can efficiently manage and complete metadata records without having to navigate between multiple applications.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to see all of the metadata for a record while I'm editing it so that I have the full context for the item I'm cataloging.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to report problems with items in the collection, so that we can track and resolve issues efficiently.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want each record to have a status so I can track the progress of the records in the collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to monitor the progress of collections, so that I can ensure timely completion and address any issues promptly.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want statuses to include a status that flags a record as having a problem so we can track issues as the arise.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to add UTF-8 encoded component files to Trident, so that users can upload and manage text files in various languages and character sets.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want Trident to support the UTF-8 character set so that I can use any character necessary when creating metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to validate and build DMRs using specified authority lists for metadata fields, so that the descriptive records are accurate and consistent with our metadata profile.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to map fields in inherited metadata to Duke Core fields and import a spreadsheet and create records.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to validate and build DMRs using specified authority lists for metadata fields, so that the records adhere to the Metadata Profile and are accurately displayed to end users.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to import metadata and create records from a DCP digitazation guide.

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Researcher, I want to be able to explore related digital resources (images, text, media) linked to an item in a collection, so that I can gain a comprehensive understanding of the context and content surrounding the item.
true_user_story: As a Archivist I want bi-directional linking between items in the digital collections and EAD finding aids

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Librarian, I want to be able to link digital collection items to external resources using pointers (URIs), so that users can easily access supplementary materials and enhance their understanding of the content.
true_user_story: As a End User I want to be able to see the physical context of an Item in a digital collection (i.e. See inside this folder; EAD)

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to monitor the progress of collections in real-time, so that I can effectively facilitate the execution of projects and ensure timely completion.
true_user_story: As a Collection Manager I want my collection to be discoverable in the Catalog

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager (DPM), I want to oversee the digitization process by grouping scannable objects into components, checking their quality/correctness, and ensuring they are added to an item by the Scanner Operator, so that the digital content is accurately and efficiently produced.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU) I want to be able to create Components.

user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU), I want to add Component files to Trident and create Administrative Metadata for each component, so that all components are properly documented and associated with a Metadata Profile.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Technical Metadata with for Component.

user_story: As a DPD, I want to create descriptive metadata records for components within an item, so that each component is accurately described and associated with the appropriate metadata profile.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Preservation Metadata for a Component.

user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD), I want to add Component files to Trident, create Administrative Metadata for each component, and generate Component derivatives, so that all components are properly cataloged and prepared for further processing.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to upload master surrogates to a Component.

user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU), I want to upload components to an item, so that I can ensure each item contains the necessary digital content.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to add one or more Components to an Item.

user_story: As a DPD, I want Trident to validate the presence of structural metadata records for items containing multiple components, so that I can ensure data integrity and adherence to quality standards.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create structural metadata for an Item so I can define the sequence of components.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager (DPM), I want to manage scanner operators and their tasks, so that the digitization process runs smoothly and efficiently.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD) I want to group components that require digitization so that I can create "digitizable units".

user_story: As a Scanner Operator, I want to be able to add components to an item after scanning materials, so that the digitized materials are correctly associated with their respective items.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to check-out/check-in groups of Components.

user_story: As a Scanner Operator, I want to be able to add components to an item, so that I can ensure the correct technical metadata is associated with each digitized material.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to verify the correctness and completeness of components so that I can ensure quality standards are met.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager (DPM), I want to manage scanner operators by adding them to the system or collection, so that I can efficiently oversee the digitization process and ensure smooth operation of the scanning tasks.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to capture digitization statistics so that I can monitor Operator efficiency and make projections for future projects.

user_story: As an Administrator, I want to be able to create Component derivatives, so that I can manage different versions of Components efficiently.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want Trident to create derivatives for me when I upload a Master.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor the progress of collections, so that I can ensure projects are executed efficiently.
true_user_story: As a User I want to monitor the progress of a digital collection project.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to manage scanner operators by adding them to the system or collection, so that I can efficiently oversee the digitization process and ensure smooth operation of the scanning tasks.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been digitized.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to manage scanner operators, perform quality control assessments of scans, and add components to items, so that the digitization process runs efficiently and meets our quality standards.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many digitized Items have been described.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to view incomplete Items, so that I can monitor and ensure timely completion of the digital collection project.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been published.♦

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to monitor the progress of digitization and description for each collection item, so that I can effectively manage and facilitate project execution.
true_user_story: As Collection Manager or Sponsor I want to describe the collection.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to validate DMRs using authority lists for metadata fields, so that the digital materials repository maintains accurate and consistent data.
true_user_story: As a Preservation Librarian I want the relationship between masters and derivatives to be managed by the system so I don't have to.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to assign metadata profiles to collections, so that the appropriate validation rules and authorities are enforced when metadata is submitted.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define the metadata schema for a collection.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to use authority lists for specific metadata fields when validating, building, editing, and displaying DMRs, so that the cataloger receives accurate guidance and the data remains consistent across collections.
true_user_story: As a Metadata librarian I want to define Authorities to be used for specific fields in a collection MD Schema.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to be able to formulate indexing rules for descriptive metadata, including which authority lists should be used for specific fields, so that the validation of metadata can accurately determine its validity and provide guidance to catalogers.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define validation rules for specific fields in a collection.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to evaluate validation rules for descriptive metadata, so that I can ensure the given metadata is valid and follows the defined schema and authority lists.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want validation rules enforced when MD is submitted.
